summary.intent: Fix SegmentGroup controlled-value edge cases identified by Codex review on PR #720, then push and get the PR approved through the full review/CI cycle.
summary.outcome: Two rounds of Codex findings were addressed: first pass resolved earlier threads, second pass (revalidation timing edge cases) was fixed with commit 'Revalidate SegmentGroup controlled selections' covering initial invalid controlled values and item-list changes that invalidate unchanged controlled values.

repo: SegmentGroup lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/segment_group/mod.rs and has both unit tests (--lib) and proptest state machine tests (--test proptest_state_machines -- selection::)
Codex review is triggered via @codex review comment on the PR and signals in-progress review with an 'eyes' reaction before posting findings
The project uses nightly rustfmt (cargo +nightly fmt --check) and enforces format checks before commit
Co-authoring commits with Codex is done via Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com> trailer

code.finding: Initial controlled props were not running through item-definition sanitization, allowing invalid initial values to pass through unchecked
When item collections change, unchanged controlled values were not requeued for validation after props sync, leaving stale selections that referenced removed/disabled items

workflow: GitHub GraphQL node IDs cannot be used directly with the numeric-ID-based review comment reply API; numeric IDs must be fetched separately
CI Clippy jobs can be slow due to workflow queue congestion rather than actual code failures — worth distinguishing before investigating
Polling loop pattern: reply+resolve threads → trigger @codex review → poll reactions+threads+checks with increasing sleep intervals (90s→120s→180s→240s)

friction: GitHub connector required numeric review comment IDs but initial thread data only had GraphQL node IDs, requiring an extra fetch step
Long CI queue delays (Clippy pending) made it hard to distinguish queue congestion from actual failures
Multiple polling rounds with manual sleep intervals were needed while waiting for Codex to complete its second review pass
open_items: Coverage, Unit Tests, Release Verification, and Dioxus feature flag CI checks were still pending at the end of the session — not confirmed green
Codex second-pass review was triggered but final approval reaction was not confirmed before the session ended
PR #720 merge was not completed — awaiting full CI green and Codex approval
